title: pseudognaphalium stramineum
intro: Pseudognaphalium stramineum, also known as brittle goldfields, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is often used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Pseudognaphalium stramineum often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote health and well-being, and may be employed to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and calm digestive issues.
